perturbation.original_text: 9.3 Payment. Any payments to be made with respect to the transfer of any Lead Compound in accordance with Section 9.1 or 9.2 above shall be immediately due to FG upon shipment, which shall be paid by Astellas to FG no later than [ * ] of the date of invoice, which invoice FG shall deliver to Astellas upon Delivery of Lead Compound to Astellas pursuant to Section 9.2(a), (b) or (c), and shall be made in U.S. dollars.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3 Payment. Any payments to be made with respect to the transfer of any Lead Compound in accordance with Section 9.1 or 9.2 above shall be immediately due to FG upon shipment, which shall be paid by Astellas to FG no later than [ * ] of the date of invoice, which invoice FG shall deliver to Astellas upon Delivery of Lead Compound to Astellas pursuant to Section 9.2(a), (b) or (c), and shall be made in U.S. dollars. Payments, however, will be considered timely if made within 90 days of the invoice date, regardless of the [ * ] timeframe.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces a contradiction by stating payments are due within the original [ * ] timeframe, but then allows for payments to be considered timely if made within 90 days. This creates uncertainty regarding the actual deadline for payments and contradicts section 10.2, where it says Any payments due under this Agreement which are not paid by the date such payments are due under this Agreement shall bear interest.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2 Payment Method. All payments under this Agreement shall be made by bank wire transfer in immediately available funds to an account designated by the payee. All such payments made by or on behalf of Astellas hereunder shall be made by a Japanese entity. All dollar amounts specified in this Agreement, and, except as specifically authorized under Section 10.3 hereof, all payments made hereunder, are and shall be made in U.S. dollars. Any payments due under this Agreement which are not paid by the date such payments are due under this Agreement shall bear interest to the extent permitted by applicable law at the U.S. prime rate per annum quoted in the "Money Rates" column of The Wall Street Journal (U.S., Western Edition) on the first business day after such payment is due, plus an additional [ * ], calculated on the number of days such payment is delinquent. This Section 10.2 shall in no way limit any other remedies available to either party.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 9.3 introduces a contradiction with Section 10.2. Section 9.3 initially states that payments are due within a specific timeframe indicated by '[ * ]'. The modified version then allows for payments to be considered timely if made within 90 days, regardless of the '[ * ]' timeframe. This directly conflicts with Section 10.2, which states that 'Any payments due under this Agreement which are not paid by the date such payments are due under this Agreement shall bear interest'. The added 90-day allowance creates uncertainty about when payments are actually 'due' under the agreement, thereby contradicting the certainty required by Section 10.2 for calculating interest on late payments. This ambiguity could lead to disputes over when interest charges apply, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10.5 Records; Inspections. Astellas shall keep, and require its Affiliates and Sublicensees to keep, complete, true and accurate books of accounts and records for the purpose of determining payments due pursuant to this Agreement. Such books and records shall be kept for at least [ * ] following the end of the calendar quarter to which they pertain. FG shall keep, and require its Sublicensee(s) to keep, complete, true and accurate books of accounts and records for the purpose of verifying the accuracy of the [ * ] Percentage and Fully Burdened Costs.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.5 Records; Inspections. Astellas shall keep, and require its Affiliates and Sublicensees to keep, complete, true and accurate books of accounts and records for the purpose of determining payments due pursuant to this Agreement. Such books and records shall be kept for at least [ * ] following the end of the calendar quarter to which they pertain. FG shall keep, and require its Sublicensee(s) to keep, complete, true and accurate books of accounts and records for the purpose of verifying the accuracy of the [ * ] Percentage and Fully Burdened Costs. Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither party is required to retain any records longer than 3 years, regardless of the [ * ] year retention period.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of the sentence "Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither party is required to retain any records longer than 3 years, regardless of the [ * ] year retention period." introduces a contradiction to the initial statement that records should be kept for at least [ * ] following the end of the calendar quarter. It contradicts the records keeping process described and can cause a dispute regarding which of the record keeping period should be the one to follow.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.5 Records; Inspections. Astellas shall keep, and require its Affiliates and Sublicensees to keep, complete, true and accurate books of accounts and records for the purpose of determining payments due pursuant to this Agreement. Such books and records shall be kept for at least [ * ] following the end of the calendar quarter to which they pertain. FG shall keep, and require its Sublicensee(s) to keep, complete, true and accurate books of accounts and records for the purpose of verifying the accuracy of the [ * ] Percentage and Fully Burdened Costs. Such records will be open for inspection at the principal place of business of each party (the "Inspected Party") during such [ * ] period by an independent auditor chosen by the other party (the "Inspecting Party") and reasonably acceptable to the Inspected Party for the purpose of verifying the amounts payable by Astellas to FG hereunder or the accuracy of the [ * ] Percentage and/or Fully Burdened Costs.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding the record retention period. The original text states that records shall be kept for at least a specific period (represented by [ * ]), while the changed text adds a clause stating that neither party is required to retain records longer than 3 years, regardless of the [ * ] year retention period. This directly contradicts the earlier statement, as it limits the retention period to 3 years even if the initial agreement specified a longer period. The contradicted text (also section 10.5) specifies that records will be open for inspection during the [ * ] period. Limiting the retention period to 3 years in the changed text directly conflicts with the ability to inspect the records for the full [ * ] period as described in the contradicted text, causing uncertainty and potential disputes over which retention period should be followed.

perturbation.original_text: 18.7.2 Termination. In the event of (a) a termination by Astellas under Section 18.6 during the period from the execution of this Agreement until the last to expire of the FG Patents, or (b) by FG under Section 18.2.1, 18.2.2, 18.2.4 or 18.2.5 hereof, Astellas shall, upon the effective date of such termination, pay to FG (i) a termination fee of $[ * ] U.S. dollars and (ii) any payments to which FG is otherwise entitled to receive hereunder in the period from the date of such termination notice until the [ * ].
perturbation.changed_text: 18.7.2 Termination. In the event of (a) a termination by Astellas under Section 18.6 during the period from the execution of this Agreement until the last to expire of the FG Patents, or (b) by FG under Section 18.2.1, 18.2.2, 18.2.4 or 18.2.5 hereof, Astellas shall, upon the effective date of such termination, pay to FG (i) a termination fee of $[ * ] U.S. dollars and (ii) any payments to which FG is otherwise entitled to receive hereunder in the period from the date of such termination notice until the [ * ]. However, If Astellas terminates the agreement due to FG's uncured breach, no termination fee is required.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence introduces a contradiction: Astellas is supposed to pay if FG is the one to terminate the contract due to an Astellas default/breach. That makes Astellas pay, but if Astellas terminates due to FG breach, the added sentences nullifys that termination fee, creating an in-text contradiction on the termination fees to be paid to FG. The term is that, in the event FG terminates due to breach, Astellas pays. The added term contradicts the earlier statement. In the event that Astellas is terminating the contract for a breach of FG, the added sentence is nullifying the prior sentence, which also means Astellas is terminating the agreement due to a term on section 18.2
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18.7.2 Termination. In the event of (a) a termination by Astellas under Section 18.6 during the period from the execution of this Agreement until the last to expire of the FG Patents, or (b) by FG under Section 18.2.1, 18.2.2, 18.2.4 or 18.2.5 hereof, Astellas shall, upon the effective date of such termination, pay to FG (i) a termination fee of $[ * ] U.S. dollars and (ii) any payments to which FG is otherwise entitled to receive hereunder in the period from the date of such termination notice until the [ * ].
perturbation.justification: The added sentence in the changed text introduces a direct contradiction to the original terms of the agreement regarding termination fees. The original text (and contradicted text, which is the same in this case) states that Astellas shall pay FG a termination fee under specific termination scenarios, including termination by FG due to Astellas's breach. The changed text adds an exception stating that if Astellas terminates due to FG's breach, no termination fee is required. This directly contradicts the earlier provision that mandates a termination fee, regardless of the reason for termination by FG under sections 18.2.1, 18.2.2, 18.2.4 or 18.2.5, creating an inconsistency as defined in the provided Perturbation Definitions.
